title: Add a unique constraint with a period aligned to the pruner
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.DailyDigestWorker` for the `:digests` queue. Give it a `unique: [period: 86_400, keys: [:user_id]]` block so one user gets at most one digest per day. Add a brief `@moduledoc` explaining that the unique period must be no longer than the `Oban.Plugins.Pruner` max_age, otherwise the unique check silently stops working as old records are pruned. perform/1 takes `%{"user_id" => id}` and calls `MyApp.Digests.build_and_send(id)`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/daily_digest_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Knowing that the pruner can delete the records the unique check relies on is a documented Elixir Forum pitfall. Sonnet usually adds the unique block but forgets the pruner relationship.
research_citation: Elixir Forum: 'Unique Oban jobs with period still seems to insert into DB' — 'I just needed to make sure the pruner didn't nuke the oban_jobs table so there was something to unique check against!'
